course.title: Introduction to AI Systems
course.scope: History, LLMs, the 2026 AI stack, ethics and safety including EU AI Act, NIST RMF, White House EO. No coding.

verdict: PASS — all five checks pass. Weeks 3 and 4 remain at 74 min (-17.8%), outside the ±15% soft-warn band but inside the ±25% hard-fail band; these are soft warns only and do not block the pass gate. Week 5 at 94 min (+4.4%), within the ok band. Three cycle-3 action items resolved in cycle 4: ACT-R1 (5.2 Worked Example replaced with clinical domain case — DUP-01 Jaccard drops below 0.45), ACT-R3 (4.7 bridge sentence added — TERM-03 resolved), ACT-R2 (3.3 forward pointer to 4.4 confirmed present in Key Takeaways). ACT-R4 (budget rebalance) and ACT-R5 (5.11 EO revocation language) remain open as soft/info items. No hard failures. course_drift_passed = true.

cycle_changes_from_prior.description: 5.2 reading.md Worked Example replaced. Prior: 2023 U.S. lawyer legal-citations case (same primary example as 3.9 corpus §7). Current: LLM-based clinical information tool evaluation — researchers found fabricated medical journal citation (journal name, publication year, author list, paraphrased conclusion) for a nonexistent study on autoimmune treatment guidelines. Domain shift: legal → medical. Jaccard re-score: estimated 0.38 (down from 0.51 in cycle 3), below the 0.45 target and well below the 0.55 hard-flag threshold. DUP-01 severity downgraded from soft-warn to resolved.

3.3 reading.md Key Takeaways forward pointer confirmed present. Final bullet reads: 'This topic introduced the agent loop in action — the five components as they run. Topic 4.4 builds on this foundation with the architectural view: how goal, LLM core, memory, tools, and planning layer are structured inside a production agent system.' Forward pointer to 4.4 is present and correctly distinguishes procedural (3.3) from architectural (4.4) framing. DUP-02 residual overlap further reduced; soft-warn retained for Jaccard ~0.52 on core concepts vocabulary (both topics cover the agent loop by design) but differentiation is now explicit.

4.7 reading.md bridge sentence confirmed present. Located at the opening of the Key Concepts section: 'In topic 3.10 you applied a rubric to judge the quality of AI outputs across five task types — creative, factual, logical, ethical, and coding. This topic applies the same scoring logic at a larger scale: judging the AI tool that produces those outputs.' This sentence distinguishes output-quality evaluation (3.10) from tool-comparison evaluation (4.7). TERM-03 resolved.

findings.resolution_text: Taxonomy bridge present at 5.2 reading.md line 93 (Key Concepts > Two types of hallucination): 'Taxonomy note: Topic 3.9 introduced a three-type system — factual, attributional, and faithful-but-wrong. Both frameworks describe the same failures: intrinsic hallucinations map to attributional errors (the model contradicts a provided source); extrinsic hallucinations cover factual invention and faithful-but-wrong cases (the model produces plausible but unsupported content). Which taxonomy you encounter depends on the source — both are in active use.' No active conflict.
Framing bridge present: 3.3 five-component procedural view and 4.4 four-component architectural view reconciled with a mapping note in 4.4 reading artifact.

Bridge sentence added at opening of Key Concepts section: 'In topic 3.10 you applied a rubric to judge the quality of AI outputs across five task types — creative, factual, logical, ethical, and coding. This topic applies the same scoring logic at a larger scale: judging the AI tool that produces those outputs.' Distinction between output-quality rubric (3.10) and tool-comparison rubric (4.7) is now explicit. TERM-03 resolved in cycle 4.

check_2_prerequisite_ordering.note: All 31 M2 topics checked. No forward-reference violations. Every topic's prerequisites reference topics with strictly earlier delivery-order positions (earlier week_num, or earlier module_position within the same week). Topics with numeric prerequisites (e.g. 4.1 has [3.2, 3.4, 3.5] as numeric not string — type inconsistency in sequence.index.json but values resolve to earlier positions and are not actionable at this critic level). Unchanged from cycle 3.

check_3_weekly_time_budget.note: Check 3 passes. Weeks 3 and 4 are soft-warn (outside ±15% band, inside ±25% hard-fail band). Week 5 is ok. No week crosses the ±25% hard-fail threshold. Soft warns do not block the pass gate per standing instruction.

3.10 (7 concepts, evaluating AI output) — upgrade standard→deep adds ~2-3 min. Secondary candidate.

per_week.note: 74 min is -17.8% below target. Outside ±15% soft-warn band (floor 76.5); inside ±25% hard-fail band (floor 67.5). Status: warn. Unchanged from cycle 3. No content fix applied to week-4 minutes. All HITL #2 profile decisions confirmed. Rebalancing requires retroactive HITL override.

per_week.note: 94 min is +4.4% above target. Within ±15% soft-warn band (76.5–103.5). Status: ok. Confirmed at 94 min (corrected from cycle-3 value of 96 min; fresh re-read of all 13 topic.manifest.json files in cycle 3 corrected this). Unchanged in cycle 4 — no minute values altered by the 5.2 Worked Example swap. No action required.

check_4_no_duplicated_material.method: Jaccard similarity on Core Concepts section vocabulary (tokenised terms, stop-words removed) of learner-facing reading artifacts

high_similarity_pairs.topic_a_title: Hallucination — what it is and why it happens
high_similarity_pairs.topic_b_title: Hallucination — why AI states falsehoods confidently
high_similarity_pairs.status: resolved — below 0.45 target and well below 0.55 hard-flag threshold
high_similarity_pairs.resolution: 5.2 reading.md Worked Example replaced in cycle 4. Prior example: 2023 U.S. lawyer legal-citations case (Mata v. Avianca) — also the primary case in 3.9 corpus §7. New example: LLM-based clinical information tool that returned a fabricated medical journal citation for a nonexistent autoimmune treatment study. Domain shift from legal to medical eliminates the primary worked-example term overlap. Remaining Jaccard (~0.38) reflects by-design structural overlap: both topics cover next-token prediction and hallucination as their shared subject, which is intentional (5.2 is the week-5 deepening of the 3.9 week-3 introduction). Taxonomy bridge in 5.2 reading.md explicitly maps the 3.9 three-type taxonomy to the 5.2 two-type taxonomy.

high_similarity_pairs.topic_b_title: Agents — LLM plus memory, tools, and a planning loop (conceptual introduction)
high_similarity_pairs.status: below hard threshold (0.55); soft warn retained. Forward pointer added in cycle 4 reduces conceptual ambiguity but does not reduce Jaccard on core vocabulary (both topics legitimately cover the agent loop).
high_similarity_pairs.residual_overlap: Both topics teach the agent loop with memory and tool use. Differentiation is now explicit: 3.3 Key Takeaways final bullet reads 'This topic introduced the agent loop in action — the five components as they run. Topic 4.4 builds on this foundation with the architectural view.' 3.3 is the historical/procedural framing; 4.4 is the architectural depth framing. Jaccard ~0.52 is by-design structural overlap for a topic pair where 4.4 is intentionally a deeper revisit of 3.3.
high_similarity_pairs.note: Soft warn retained for tracking. Not a hard failure. No action required to pass.

scope_coverage.facet: History of AI (symbolic AI → ML → deep learning → LLMs → agents)
LLMs — what they are, tokens, training, inference, temperature/sampling
AI in context — India deployments, jagged frontier, hallucination, evaluation
2026 AI stack — foundation models, fine-tuning, RAG, agents, tool use, multimodal
AI tool comparison and evidence-based recommendation
Ethics and safety — real failures, data bias, four pillars, red-teaming, prompt injection
EU AI Act — risk tiers, high-risk obligations, prohibited uses (Article 5)
NIST AI Risk Management Framework (Govern, Map, Measure, Manage)
White House Executive Order on AI (EO 14110, 2023)
scope_coverage.note: ACT-R5 open: confirm 5.11 reading artifact explicitly states EO 14110 was revoked January 2025. concepts_introduced includes 'revocability of executive action' — reading artifact must deliver on that with current-status clarity for the 2026 cohort. Scope facet is covered; the open item is a content-accuracy verification, not an arc gap.

scope_coverage.facet: India AI governance (MEITY advisory, global framework alignment)

scope_coverage.note: Verified. No reading artifact teaches code syntax. 3.4 corpus Implementation section includes illustrative code snippets as author reference material only — the learner-facing reading artifact is the governed artifact, not the corpus.
